question: Is the bar countertop material changed to white marble?
Does the new countertop surface display gray veining patterns?
Does the countertop surface appear to have a polished, glossy finish?
Is the marble veining pattern natural and non-repetitive (avoiding tiling artifacts)?
Does the marble texture follow the correct perspective and depth of the counter?
Are reflections of the overhead lights visible on the new glossy countertop surface?
Do objects resting on the counter (e.g., espresso machine, pastry domes) cast realistic shadows onto the new surface?
Is the boundary between the man's arm and the countertop sharp and free of blending artifacts?
Is the original shape and edge geometry of the countertop preserved?
Are the wooden wall panels and wine rack in the background preserved?
Are the brass pendant lamps preserved in appearance and position?
Are the espresso machines and coffee equipment on the counter preserved?
Are the glass pastry domes and their contents preserved without alteration?
Are smaller items on the counter (e.g., cups, newspaper) preserved?
Is the edited area free of blurriness or resolution loss compared to the rest of the image?
Is the edit confined to the countertop without color bleeding or texture spilling onto adjacent objects?
